spec_match.rationale: Demonstrates the pairing the chromestatus summary explicitly calls out ('possibly in combination with the Compute Pressure API'), but uses a hand-rolled `hardwareConcurrency >= 8 && memory >= 8 → high` rule rather than calling `navigator.cpu.performance()` — the page is named for the new API but never actually reads it.

interactive.rationale: Live PressureObserver when available, simulated chips when not, a moving pressure history canvas, a highlighted decision-matrix row that recomputes on every pressure change — a strong interactive surface.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb promises 'live dashboard, decision matrix mapping tier × pressure to quality, automatic stepping on saturation'. The page delivers all three with the auto-simulation fallback when PressureObserver is missing.

edge_cases.rationale: Covers all four pressure states and three tiers, including a graceful PressureObserver-missing fallback. But no demo of: rapid state oscillation (hysteresis), state change while observer is paused, the 'audio-only' floor actually swapping a video element for an audio stream, or PermissionsPolicy disabling compute-pressure.

references.rationale: The see-also section only links to sibling concepts and does NOT include the chromestatus.com/feature/5189864286978048 URL, breaking the self-heal invariant in CLAUDE.md #3.

design_system.rationale: Mostly variable-driven, but inline hex colours (`#88bb00`, `#f5a623`, `#edfdf0`, `#fdf8e8`, `#fde8e8`, `#b87b00`) appear in pressure-fill / quality-badge styles — violating the 'CSS variables, never raw hex' rule in CLAUDE.md #5. The chart canvas also paints `#0022ff` directly.

openQuestions.title: Replace the heuristic with a real navigator.cpu.performance() call
openQuestions.detail: This page exists to demonstrate the CPU Performance API. It currently fakes the tier from hardwareConcurrency + deviceMemory. Read `navigator.cpu.performance()`, fall back to the heuristic only if undefined, and show both side by side so visitors see what the new API adds.

openQuestions.title: Add the chromestatus.com link
openQuestions.detail: The see-also section only links to sibling concepts. Add `https://chromestatus.com/feature/5189864286978048` so the page satisfies the self-heal invariant (CLAUDE.md #3).

openQuestions.title: Replace inline hex colours with CSS variables
openQuestions.detail: Pressure-fair `#88bb00`, pressure-serious `#f5a623`, highlighted-row `#edfdf0`, quality-mid `#fdf8e8`, quality-low `#fde8e8`, chart stroke `#0022ff` are all raw hex. Either add semantic variables to `public/styles.css` (--pressure-fair, --pressure-serious) or recolour to existing accent-* variables.

openQuestions.title: Surface hysteresis
openQuestions.detail: Real PressureObserver streams can oscillate rapidly between fair and serious. The page should show the recommendation NOT actually changing on every tick — there is a debounce / hysteresis pattern in the spec discussion. Add a 'enable hysteresis (3-sample window)' toggle that visually delays the response so authors learn the pattern.

summary: Excellent interactive dashboard and decision matrix, but two regressions: it never actually calls navigator.cpu.performance() — the feature whose page this is — and it ships without the chromestatus link plus a sprinkle of raw hex colours. Fix those and it becomes the canonical demo for tier × pressure pairing.
